temporary_upload_object.title: chatgpt-export-2026-07-22T01-35-30.jsonl.2.crswap
temporary_upload_object.proof_boundary: The non-zero size proves a large Drive object exists. The temporary suffix, zero-byte final placeholder, and failed authorised download routes prevent parseability, hashing, completeness, or conversation-count claims.

counting_rule: The final JSONL and attachment ZIP form one export generation. A temporary .crswap object is not counted as a second export and does not establish a conversation denominator until its bytes are accessible, hashable, parseable, and reconciled.
exact_next_action: Allow Google Drive to finalise the upload so the .crswap object is replaced by a non-zero final JSONL. Because the available Drive download route has a 100 MB limit and the object is 2.56 GB, provide a compressed multipart copy with each part below 100 MB if the final file remains inaccessible through the connector.
truth_boundary: The Drive folder is fully enumerated within its three direct children. Attachments are verified; the new conversation ledger remains unprocessed because only a zero-byte final placeholder and an inaccessible temporary upload object are available.
